name: The fall of the Western Roman Empire
summary: Odoacer deposed Romulus Augustulus, the last Western Roman emperor, marking the traditional end of the Western Roman Empire.

name: Odoacer Becomes King of Italy
summary: After deposing the last Roman emperor, Odoacer declared himself King of Italy, establishing a Germanic rule over former Roman territories.

name: Roman Imperial Regalia Sent to Constantinople
summary: Odoacer sent the imperial insignia of the Western Roman Empire to Emperor Zeno in Constantinople, symbolizing the end of separate Western imperial authority.

name: Visigothic Kingdom Consolidates Power in Hispania
summary: Following the collapse of Western Roman authority, the Visigoths strengthened their control over Hispania, establishing a dominant regional kingdom.

name: Burgundian Kingdom Expands in Gaul
summary: The Burgundians expanded their territory in southeastern Gaul, taking advantage of the weakening Roman presence in the region.

name: Eastern Roman Empire Maintains Stability Under Zeno
summary: While the Western Empire collapsed, Emperor Zeno maintained control in the Eastern Roman Empire, preserving Roman governance in the east.

name: Anglo-Saxon Settlement Expands in Britain
summary: Germanic tribes, including the Anglo-Saxons, continued expanding their settlements in Britain, accelerating the decline of Roman influence.
